etymology.description: 品(品) (pǐn, "three mouths") + 木(木) (mù, "tree") = chirping of birds in a tree
etymology.interpretation: 喿(zào) combines three repetitions of 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") to emphasize vocalization, set above 木(木) (mù, "tree"), to depict the sound of birds chirping in and around a tree. This character originally depicted the idea of chirping and was the original form for 噪(噪) (zào, "noise; chirp"), further emphasizing this auditory aspect.
